pi_items.source: Path specification - verification requires task execution
Path specification - verification requires task execution

Direct DICOM analysis of 108 slices from official CT Cranium dataset. Bone voxels (HU 226-3071) counted and volume calculated: 1,901,372 voxels × 4.58e-4 cm³/voxel = 870.7 cm³. Metadata threshold is conservative and verified.

Mathematical analysis of expected morphological dilation. For skull with ~871 cm³ volume (estimated radius 5.92 cm, surface area 441 cm²), 1-voxel dilation (0.048 cm shell) should add ~21.2 cm³, resulting in ratio of 1.0243. Metadata threshold is conservative and verified as achievable.
Downloaded and extracted successfully. 24.3 MB zip file containing DICOM dataset from official InVesalius 3 GitHub releases.

Verified as standard clinical bone segmentation threshold. Actual dataset HU range: [-1024, 3033], consistent with standard CT imaging. 4.46% of all pixels fall within bone HU range across the dataset.
DICOM PatientName tag extracted from sample file. This is a publicly available sample dataset, not a real patient study.
Counted DICOM files in extracted dataset. 108 sequential CT slices with 512×512 pixel resolution.
Extracted from DICOM PixelSpacing and SliceThickness tags. Results in voxel volume of 4.58e-4 cm³.

privileged_info_summary: The CT Cranium dataset is real clinical imaging data (patient identifier: 0051) from official InVesalius 3 releases. The dataset contains 108 DICOM slices with 0.4785 mm in-plane and 2.0 mm slice thickness resolution. Bone volume at standard clinical threshold (226-3071 HU) measures approximately 870.7 cm³, confirming the min_volume_cm3 threshold of 500.0 cm³ is conservative. The dilation_ratio_threshold of 1.02 is mathematically verified to be achievable with standard 1-voxel morphological dilation, which should produce approximately 1.024× volume expansion for this anatomical structure.
